question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to a ContractRoutes endpoint and exploit the fact that the extendExpress wrap() function's _once() guard prevents next() from being called more than once, so that if recordRequestPath calls next() and then the route handler also tries to call next(), the second call is silently dropped, potentially causing the responseHandler to never run and fee data to never be sent?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to /contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash where transactionIdOrHash is a Hedera transaction ID (0.0.X-timestamp-nonce format), and exploit the fact that recordRequestPath sets requestPathLabel to /api/v1/contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash, which may have a different cache-control TTL than the hash-based lookup, causing fee data to be cached differently based on the lookup method?
